product_overview: RiskNode is a lightweight frontend tool for amateur crypto traders to deterministically calculate maximum position size from capital, risk percentage, entry and stop-loss prices, and to persist those calculations locally in a trade journal for later review.
core_goals: Deterministically calculate position size using the exact formula: Position Size = (Total Capital * (Risk% / 100)) / |Entry - Stop Loss|
Enforce input constraints: prevent division by zero, reject risk percentages above 5%, and block negative numeric inputs
Persist trade entries as an array of JSON objects in localStorage with the defined object structure (id, date, asset_pair, capital, risk, entry_price, stop_loss_price, position_size, status)
Provide basic journal management: mark trades Won/Lost, delete single entries, and clear the entire journal
Keep the application client-side only (no backend/auth) and provide clear validation feedback to the user

features.name: Risk Calculation
features.description: Allow users to input capital, risk %, entry price and stop loss price on the Risk Calculator page to compute position size deterministically and save valid calculations to the Journal.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Enter capital in data-testid="input-modal" -> Enter risk % in data-testid="input-risiko" (<=5%) -> Enter entry price in data-testid="input-entry" -> Enter stop loss price in data-testid="input-stoploss" (different from entry) -> See calculated position size automatically -> Click "Save to Journal" button (data-testid="btn-calculate") -> Navigate to /journal -> See new entry in journal with calculated position_size

Navigate to / -> Enter capital in data-testid="input-modal" -> Enter risk % in data-testid="input-risiko" (<=5%) -> Enter entry price in data-testid="input-entry" -> Enter stop loss price equal to entry price in data-testid="input-stoploss" -> See error message "Invalid price distance" -> See "Save to Journal" button (data-testid="btn-calculate") disabled

Navigate to / -> Enter capital in data-testid="input-modal" -> Enter risk % in data-testid="input-risiko" (>5%) -> See validation error "Risk must be 5% or less" -> See "Save to Journal" button (data-testid="btn-calculate") disabled
Navigate to / -> Enter negative capital in data-testid="input-modal" -> See validation error "Values must not be negative" -> See "Save to Journal" button (data-testid="btn-calculate") disabled

features.name: Journal Management
features.description: Persist, display and manage trade entries stored in localStorage from the Journal page. Support marking trades as Won/Lost and removing single or all entries.
user_flows: Navigate to /journal -> See list of saved calculations and trade entries populated from localStorage -> Click Mark trade as Won using data-testid="btn-won" on an entry -> See entry status updated to "Won" in the list -> See updated object persisted in localStorage

Navigate to /journal -> See list of saved calculations and trade entries populated from localStorage -> Click Delete trade entry using data-testid="btn-delete" on an entry -> See entry removed from the list -> See entry removed from localStorage

Navigate to /journal -> See list of saved calculations and trade entries populated from localStorage -> Click Clear entire journal using data-testid="btn-delete-all" -> See journal list becomes empty -> See localStorage journal key cleared or set to []
Navigate to /journal when no entries exist -> See empty state indicating no saved trades -> Attempt to mark trade as Won using data-testid="btn-won" (no effect) -> See no change and no error thrown

routes.description: Risk Calculator page where users input capital, risk, and price levels.
Trading Journal page displaying saved calculations and trade outcomes.

features.name: Risk Calculation
features.description: Deterministically calculate position size based on capital, risk percentage, entry price, and stop loss price.
user_interactions: Enter stop loss price in data-testid="input-stoploss"

features.name: Journal Management
features.description: Persist and manage trade entries in local storage.
user_interactions: Mark trade as Won using data-testid="btn-won"
Mark trade as Lost using data-testid="btn-lost"
